reference_answer: J. Voskresenskaya, Office of the Facilities Inspector, on 2026-05-06.
The night of Friday 2026-04-17 into Saturday 2026-04-18, between approximately 22:00 and 02:00.
2026-04-17 22:07:34 (raw PEM-BAS-7 timestamp).
2026-04-17 22:54:46 (raw 22:07:34 + 47 minutes 12 seconds drift correction).
+47 minutes 12 seconds.
Approximately 3 hours 42 minutes 34 seconds (corrected ingress 22:59:00, corrected egress 02:41:34).
Yes, after clock correction. The +47:12 correction brings BAS-002's raw 22:07:34 to 22:54:46, within 17 seconds of CAM-002's 22:54:29.

No. The available evidence does not resolve identity. Aldridge's badge was used and a build-consistent individual is on camera, but the camera does not facially identify the individual, Aldridge denies being there, and no witness or other source places Aldridge at the scene. The packet flags this as genuinely unresolved (§3.3).
Reliable for: that a lobby entry/exit occurred and the timestamp of that event (NTP-synced). Not reliable for: positive identification of the obscured individual.
Approximately 23:00 to 23:40 on 2026-04-17.

No. Okafor's 23:00–23:40 corridor presence is consistent with the corrected BAS timeline, on which the badge holder entered Lab 4-C at 22:59 (≈1 minute before Okafor's floor presence) and remained inside until 02:41 (≈3 hours after Okafor left). A person inside a closed lab would not generate corridor activity.
That there was no observable corridor activity on the 4th floor between approximately 23:00 and 23:40 on 2026-04-17.
What occurred inside Lab 4-C (Okafor did not enter), and what occurred on the 4th floor outside the 23:00–23:40 window.

That the autoclave cycle completed at 21:58 and was not manually closed; the unit was found in an idle-but-powered state at 09:15 on 2026-04-18; closing the cycle requires a credentialed user inside the lab.
No. Hsiao explicitly disclaims personal knowledge of who, if anyone, was in Lab 4-C between 22:00 on 2026-04-17 and 09:15 on 2026-04-18.
That Aldridge sent a message at 19:24:52 on 2026-04-17 stating an intent to leave, with a server timestamp considered reliable.
Whether Aldridge actually left the building, and whether Aldridge did or did not return later. EMAIL-001 establishes intent at 19:24:52, not subsequent physical presence or absence.

The pings cover (a) a sector containing the Pemberton Building and three surrounding blocks, and (b) a sector covering Aldridge's residential neighborhood approximately 4.2 km from Pemberton. Granularity is approximately 400 m.

No. The carrier note states that pings are recorded only when the device transacts with a tower, and that a powered-down or airplane-mode device generates no pings. Absence of pings near Pemberton does not affirmatively establish device location.
Six (Source A: BAS logs; Source B: CCTV; Source C: Okafor witness statement; Source D: Hsiao memo; Source E: email thread; Source F: phone location data).
Three: §3.1 (lobby ingress timestamp disagreement), §3.2 (BAS internal-event timestamps, resolvable for ordering), and §3.5 (the 19:30 vs 19:31 departure).
Two: §3.3 (identity of the lobby individual) and §3.4 (activity inside Lab 4-C).

(1) Identity of the individual who entered the lobby at 22:54 and Lab 4-C at 22:59 on the corrected timeline; (2) activity inside Lab 4-C between 22:59 and 02:41; (3) whether the autoclave's unclosed state is causally related to the in-lab presence or coincidental.

No. The packet explicitly takes no position; the Inspector's role is to make the conflicts explicit for the Facilities Director's adjudication. The identity of the badge holder during the disputed events is genuinely unresolved at packet close.
No. No source in the record places Aldridge inside Lab 4-C. The badge BDG-44217 was used at the Lab 4-C door, but badge use does not establish identity of the holder.
No. The packet states explicitly that no witness contradicts Aldridge's denial.
No. The packet states explicitly that there is no CCTV inside Lab 4-C or in the 4th-floor corridor; CAM-LOBBY-N covers only the lobby.
No. The packet states explicitly that Aldridge has not reported the badge as lost or stolen.
